output.change: Added [&.scrolling]:mt-0 to .header-main in TSX and PHP; updated HeaderMain.js showSpacer calls to include marginTop in height calculation

code1_scrollingMt0_tsx.detail: Line 498: base class string is 'header-main bg-charcoal rounded-[25px] px-8 py-4 w-full mt-8 [&.scrolling]:mt-0 transition-[transform,opacity]'. Both mt-8 (normal state) and [&.scrolling]:mt-0 (fixed state) are present.

code2_scrollingMt0_php.detail: Line 59: base class string is 'header-main bg-charcoal rounded-[25px] px-8 py-4 w-full mt-8 [&.scrolling]:mt-0 transition-[transform,opacity]'. Exact match with TSX.

code3_jsMarginTopInSpacer.description: HeaderMain.js showSpacer calls include marginTop from getComputedStyle before .scrolling is added
code3_jsMarginTopInSpacer.detail: Two call sites verified. (1) updateScrollStatus line 174: 'const headerMarginTop = parseInt( window.getComputedStyle( header ).marginTop, 10 ) || 0;' followed by line 175: 'showSpacer( header.offsetHeight + headerMarginTop );' — reads marginTop before header.classList.add('scrolling') on line 177, capturing the pre-fixed mt-8 value (32px). (2) openMenu line 298: 'const headerMarginTopOpen = parseInt( window.getComputedStyle( header ).marginTop, 10 ) || 0;' followed by line 303: 'showSpacer( header.offsetHeight + headerMarginTopOpen );' — same pattern applied when mobile menu forces fixed mode. Both sites read marginTop before the scrolling class is applied, ensuring the spacer correctly reserves the full displaced height (header height + margin) so no layout shift occurs.

frontend1_normalStateMargin.description: Header in non-scrolled state still has visible top margin
frontend1_normalStateMargin.detail: Full-page screenshots at all four breakpoints confirm every header variation has clear top margin above the nav bar pill inside its demo container. The mt-8 (32px) on .header-main is intact and visually evident — the rounded dark pill does not sit flush against the demo container top edge.

frontend2_fixedNoDoubledSpacing.description: When scrolled and fixed, no doubled spacing — header sits at top with appropriate offset only
frontend2_fixedNoDoubledSpacing.detail: Scroll-state screenshot at 1440px (scrolled to y=2000) shows the fixed header sitting cleanly at the top of the viewport. The nav bar pill starts immediately at the container edge with no extra gap above it — [&.scrolling]:mt-0 has removed the mt-8. The spacer below the original header position reserves the correct height (offsetHeight + marginTop captured before .scrolling was added), preventing a layout jump when the header lifts out of flow. No doubled spacing observed.

frontend3_overlayMode.detail: The 'DISPLAY ABOVE CONTENT (OVERLAY MODE)' variation visible in all breakpoint screenshots. Header overlays Lorem ipsum content, content not pushed down. The [&.scrolling]:mt-0 does not interfere with overlay mode because: (a) in the initial absolute-positioned state the header already has mt-8 for visual offset from container top, and (b) when scrolling triggers .scrolling class the mt-0 override removes the margin as designed — overlay mode spacer logic is separately gated by '! displayAboveContent' in both showSpacer call sites in JS.

frontend4_mobileFixed.detail: At 375px all mobile-layout header variations render correctly. The JS openMenu path (line 297-305) forces fixed mode on mobile when fixedScrollMobile is set and the menu opens — this path also uses the marginTop-inclusive showSpacer call. The [&.scrolling]:mt-0 class applies at max-md breakpoint via the unconditional base string, so mobile fixed headers also lose the mt-8 gap when in scrolling state, consistent with the desktop behaviour.

passedChecks: TSX .header-main base class includes both mt-8 and [&.scrolling]:mt-0 (line 498)
PHP .header-main base class includes both mt-8 and [&.scrolling]:mt-0 (line 59)
TSX and PHP .header-main base class strings are identical
JS updateScrollStatus: reads getComputedStyle(header).marginTop before adding .scrolling, passes offsetHeight + marginTop to showSpacer (lines 174-175)
JS openMenu: same marginTop-inclusive showSpacer pattern applied when forcing mobile fixed mode (lines 298, 303)
Non-scrolled headers have visible top margin at all breakpoints
Scrolled/fixed header sits at viewport top with no doubled gap above pill
Overlay mode unaffected — displayAboveContent gate in JS prevents spacer from running
Mobile layout correct at 375px across all variations
12 final screenshots captured across all browsers and breakpoints

supplementaryScreenshots.purpose: Confirms no doubled spacing in scrolled/fixed state at desktop width

visualObservations.375px: Mobile layout. All 10 header variations render correctly. Nav bar pill has visible top margin above it (mt-8 intact). SEP mobile button above nav bar when configured. Overlay variation near bottom shows header over Lorem ipsum. No layout regressions.
visualObservations.768px: Tablet, below the 960px breakpoint so mobile layout applies. Hamburger toggles visible, nav collapsed. Header pill margin consistent across all variations.
visualObservations.1024px: Desktop layout (above 960px). Full utility row with search box and SEP link. Nav items in horizontal row. All variations correct including overlay. Consistent mt-8 top spacing on all nav bar pills in non-scrolled state.
visualObservations.1440px: Full desktop. Non-scrolled: all 10 variations show mt-8 gap above nav bar pill. Overlay mode confirmed. Scrolled (supplementary screenshot): fixed header sits at viewport top with zero gap above pill — [&.scrolling]:mt-0 correctly eliminating the mt-8 so no doubled spacing.
